channel.title: Cake Frosting on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Cake Frosting on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Royal Icing
item.description:
• 3 level Tbsp meringue powder
  • 500 gr (or 1.10 lb) sifted icing sugar
  • 3-1/2 oz warm water
  • ½ tsp cream of tartar

Combine ingredients, mixing slowly. Then beat at medium speed for 7 – 10 minutes. Utensils have to be grease-free. Icing dries rapidly so keep it covered with a damp cloth. To restore texture, re-beat slowly. Makes 3-1/2 cups.

OR:

Ingredients

  • 3 egg whites
  • 500 gr. (1.10 lb) sifted icing sugar
  • 1 tsp almond flavoring

Instructions

Whip at low speed till desired working consistency is reached. Keep covered with damp cloth while working flowers. Store tightly covered in fridge. Whip again before using.

item.title: Simple Caramel Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup lightly salted butter
  • 1-1/2 cups dark brown sugar
  • 1/3 cup milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 3 cups icing sugar

Instructions

  1.  Melt butter in small pan and stir in brown sugar.

  2.  Stir in milk and bring mixture just to a boil.

  3.  Cool to lukewarm. Stir in vanilla and icing sugar and beat until creamy.

Credit to Country Living for this recipe!

item.title: Rich Chocolate Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 squares unsweetened chocolate
  • 1 cup icing sugar
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Melt chocolate in top of double boiler. Stir in icing sugar, beaten egg, butter and vanilla. Remove from heat at once and stir. Cool a little before spreading on brownies.

item.title: Rich Butter Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup butter
  • 2-1/2 or 2-3/4 cup sifted icing sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 egg

Instructions

Cream butter well; stir in egg. Add sugar, a little at a time, beating until light and fluffy. Stir in vanilla.

Yield: enough for 2 pans.

item.title: Quick and Easy Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 envelope whipped topping mix (2 to 2-1/2 cup yield)
  • 1 pkg instant pudding mix (4 serving size)
  • 1-1/2 cup cold milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla, if desired

Instructions

In a chilled bowl, blend and whip topping mix, instant pudding, cold milk and vanilla until stiff (3-8 min.). Immediately frost cake and store in refrigerator. Serve chilled. Frosted cake may be frozen for storage

item.title: Miracle Icing
item.description:
WHITE SAUCE;

  • 1 cup milk
  • 4 tbsp flour

Cook as for white sauce, and let cool.

BEAT WELL;

Ingredients

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla.

Instructions

When sauce is cooled, add the butter and sugar mixture, and BEAT WELL.

Makes enough for a layer cake.

item.title: Marshmallow Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 1/3 cup water
  • Pinch of cream of tartar
  • Pinch salt
  • 2 egg whites
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Combine sugar, water, cream of tartar and salt in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, stir to dissolve sugar and continue to cook, without stirring, until mixture reaches soft ball stage of 240 F./115C.

Place egg whites in a bowl and beat with an electric mixer until white and frothy. Continue to beat, adding sugar mixture very slowly, until very light and fluffy. Beat in vanilla.

item.title: Fluffy Chocolate Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups icing sugar
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 6 tablespoons butter
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • 4 squares bitter chocolate
  • ½ cup milk
  • 2 eggs

Instructions

Blend sugar, milk, eggs, salt and vanilla in a bowl over ice water. Melt chocolate and butter together. Add while warm to first mixture and beat until desired consistency.

item.title: Jell-O Seven Minute Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 egg whites, unbeaten
  • 1-1/4 cups sugar
  • 1 pkg Jell-O (any flavour)
  • Dash of salt
  • 1/3 cup water
  • 2 tsp light corn syrup

Instructions

Combine all ingredients in top of double boiler. Beat 1 minute to mix thoroughly. Place over boiling water and beat constantly with rotary beater (or at high speed of electric mixer) 7 minutes, or until frosting will stand in stiff peaks. (Stir frosting up from bottom and sides of pan occasionally.) Remove from boiling water. Beat 1 minute, or until thick enough to spread.

item.title: Dot's Flour Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5 Tbsp flour
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup butter
  • ½ cup shortening
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp lemon extract or vanilla

Instructions

Mix flour, milk and egg and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until mixture thickens.

Cool. Combine sugar, butter and shortening, and then blend in cooled mixture. Beat until smooth and fluffy; mix in flavoring and spread on cake.

item.title: Creamy Strawberry Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup butter, shortening or margarine
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp lemon juice
  • 1 tsp (1/2 of a small packet) Strawberry unsweetened instant soft drink mix
  • 3-1/2 cups sifted icing sugar
  • ¼ cup milk

Instructions

Combine Crisco, salt, lemon juice, dry strawberry instant drink mix and 1 cup sugar.

Add milk and remaining sugar alternately and mix until smooth. Add milk or sugar as necessary for good spreading consistency.

item.title: Cream Cheese Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 4-oz pkg cream cheese
  • 2 cups icing sugar
  • 2 tbsp milk
  • ¼ tsp salt

Instructions

In beater bowl beat cheese until creamy then add sugar and salt and beat until smooth, adding milk slowly until of smooth almost runny spreading consistency.

item.title: Buttercream Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup Crisco (or ½ cup Crisco + ½ cup white margarine)
  • 1 kg (2.20 lbs) icing sugar
  • ½ cup water (I use a wee bit more – in icing I use to ice the cake – it’s easier to spread)
  • 1 tsp almond flavoring (I use 2 – 3 tsp of almond or coconut flavor)

Instructions

This icing is the easiest recipe for doing cakes. It has a creamy texture and is great to practice making flowers. And for the borders – just add a few drops of water.
